title: libpod: allow to configure path to the slirp4netns binary
body:
allow to configure the path to the slirp4netns binary, either via an
option flag --slirp4netns or through the libpod.conf configuration
file.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2506

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Instead of hard coding the name slirp4netns into the option, could we call it network-cmd or something, that way if a future network-cmd comes along to replace slirp4netns or has different features, we can support it.

So just to make sure I'm on the same page, are you thinking then that it would look like: `network-cmd=slirp4netns` then and later we could add valid values as the became available?  I like the thought, but maybe `--rootless-network`?
Well can I use slirp4netns inside of a locked down container?  I am not sure this requires rootless.
I believe we allow it, though I'm not certain why anyone would want to.

yes we allow slirp4netns for root containers, it is slow but on the other hand it doesn't require to tweak the host configuration.  So it could be useful for containers that seldomly need to communicate with the outside world

> Instead of hard coding the name slirp4netns into the option, could we call it network-cmd or something, that way if a future network-cmd comes along to replace slirp4netns or has different features, we can support it.

I'd rather see slirp4netns disappear than adding new ones.  This should be handled by CNI at some point
Then lets not embed the name into the CLI.
changed to `--network-cmd-path` and `network_cmd_path` in the `libpod.conf` file
@mheon, good idea.  I've addressed your comment and pushed a new version

I'm not sure it makes sense to hide `slirp4netns` name here, because non-slirp4netns "network cmd" would still need to provide slirp4netns-compatible CLI?

> I'd rather see slirp4netns disappear than adding new ones. This should be handled by CNI at some point

Maybe you already noticed, there is a CNI plugin for slirp4netns: https://github.com/mgoltzsche/slirp-cni-plugin

> > 
> 
> Maybe you already noticed, there is a CNI plugin for slirp4netns: https://github.com/mgoltzsche/slirp-cni-plugin

no, I've not seen that.  That is great, I'll need to look into rootless CNI

> I'm not sure it makes sense to hide `slirp4netns` name here, because non-slirp4netns "network cmd" would still need to provide slirp4netns-compatible CLI?

I guess it depends from the context.  When we run a `slirp4netns` network then the `network-cmd` refers to slirp4netns.  When it is used from other contexts then it will have a different meaning.
